steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says those volcanoes look like a shield lying flat. The second sentence says volcanologists classify them as 'shield volcanoes.' The transition must express that the name is a fitting match for the appearance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Still,’: Signals contrast or concession — but there is no contrast here, only a logical follow-on. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Besides,’: Adds a separate or additional point — the classification is not an unrelated addition, it directly follows from the appearance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Second,’: Implies this is the second item in a list, but no first item was introduced. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Fittingly,’: Signals that what follows is an apt or appropriate consequence — the name 'shield volcano' is fitting precisely because they look like shields. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
